subject: Geology
title: Hardness and Mohs Scale
description: A micro-lesson that makes Hardness and Mohs Scale usable.

hook.text: A fingernail scratches gypsum. A steel knife scratches feldspar. Hardness is a mineral's resistance to scratching.

buildup.text: Friedrich Mohs ranked ten minerals from softest to hardest in 1812, creating the Mohs hardness scale.

discovery.text: Talc is 1, quartz is 7, and diamond is 10. Each mineral scratches those below it on the scale.

twist.text: The scale isn't linear. Diamond is only one step above corundum, but it's actually four times harder.

climax.text: Hardness is one of the fastest ways to identify a mineral in the field. All you need is a scratch test.

punchline.text: Scratch it and you'll know what it is.

quiz.question: What does the Mohs scale measure?
